session_focus: Verify GUI training data is real and fix autonomous loop write lock to allow any agent to work on current task.
user_directives: User asked if the 13.93K tokens and 31 trace artifacts shown in GUI are real training data before building deeper.
User asked if the autonomous loop log is fixed.
User complained about lock-based failures: 'we cant create failure paramters just based on who the llm is thats retarded'.
User demanded: 'make it open allowable for any agent to write the single task'.
assistant_responses: Confirmed YES - real training data with instruction/input/output/signal tuples from actual sessions.
Identified lock problem: expired lock file wasn't clearing, same-task continuation wasn't allowed.
Fixed security_manager.py acquire_global_write_lock() to allow expired lock takeover and same-task refresh.
Cleared stale lock file, committed and pushed fix.
training_value: Training data should be verified as real before building UI features on top of it.
Write locks should be task-scoped, not agent-scoped - any LLM/agent working on the same task should be able to continue writing.
Expired locks must auto-release to prevent blocking.
Lock failures based on 'which agent' rather than 'which task' create artificial failures that waste cycles.
The single-write constraint should be about task consistency, not agent identity.
